MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JULY 14, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. Off Calendar Sep-04-2014 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Feb-24-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant And Cross-Complainant Farmers New World Life Insurance Company'S Motion For An Order Of Dismissal Of Defendant And Cross-Complainant Farmers New World Life Insurance Company And For An Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Aug-04-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES J. FICENEC OF THE ARCHER NORRIES LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (925) 930-6600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT THOMAS BURKE;
ATTY. CHRISTOPHER J. BENNINGTON OF THARPE & HOWELL, LLP, TEL. NO. (818) 205-9955 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT FARMERS NEW WORLD LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS PREVIOUS TENTATIVE RULING ISSUED ON JULY 30, 2014 AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT FARMERS NEW WORLD LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For An Order Of Dismissal And For An Award Of Attorney's Fees And Costs - The motion is granted. Farmers New World Like Insurance Co. is dismissed from this action and is entitled to $5,000 as reasonable fees in pursuit of the interpleader. See CCP 386.5 and 386.6. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312). Judge: John Stewart; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Brenda L. Crow, CSR #10503, Tel. No. (916)872-7488 =302/JKS

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED REGARDING FEES. THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT FARMERS NEW WORLD LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For An Order Of Dismissal Of Defendant And Cross-Complainant Farmers New World Life Insurance Company And For An Award Of Attorney's Fees And Costs - The motion is granted. Farmers New World Like Insurance Co. is dismissed from this action and is entitled to $7,500.00 as reasonable fees in pursuit of the interpleader. See CCP 386.5 and 386.6.
